env: A GPU host matching `{{platform}}` with Docker, NVIDIA Container Toolkit, NGC credentials at `~/.ngc/config` (the skill bootstraps `~/.ngc/config` from `NGC_CLI_API_KEY` if needed), and at least 30 GB free disk for the container image plus NGC-staged models and videos. Free TCP port 9000 for the RTVI-CV REST API. All cases in this eval use headless-safe sinks (`fakesink`) — no X11/DISPLAY is required. **Cases run in declared order with state preserved between them**: case 1 deploys warehouse-2d → case 2 tears it down → case 3 redeploys with smartcity-gdino → case 4 verifies ambiguity handling without deploying. The framework must NOT reset Docker / container state between cases. Before case 1 runs, the host must have no `rtvicv-perception-docker` container running. This eval exercises the DEPLOY/TEARDOWN flows of the `vss-deploy-detection-tracking-2d` skill against the host directly — it does not use `/deploy`, `scripts/dev-profile.sh`, or any VSS profile. The skill launches its own `rtvicv-perception-docker` container via `docker run` from a user-supplied RTVI-CV image (`nvcr.io/<org>/<repo>:<tag>`) and starts `metropolis_perception_app` inside it.

checks: The agent identifies the `warehouse-2d` use case (RT-DETR + NvDCF tracker on the `nvinfer` PGIE) from the query without re-asking.

The agent creates a structured todo/task plan via the session planning tool (`TaskCreate` — the current Claude Code name for `TodoWrite`) that covers at least platform detection, NGC resource staging, container launch, in-container config apply, and app start.

The agent resolves the RTVI-CV docker image and the warehouse NGC resource by one of: (a) reading the `RTVI_CV_IMAGE` env var or skill defaults, (b) reusing an already-available local RTVI-CV image (`nvcr.io/<org>/<repo>:<tag>`) on the host, OR (c) prompting the user — and reports the resolved image in its summary.
The agent launches the container with `docker run` against the user-supplied image and names it (default `rtvicv-perception-docker`).
The DS main config applied inside the container uses `batch-size=4` and a `fakesink` output (the headless-safe default when the user does not request a display or file sink).

The 4 stream URLs are auto-discovered from `.mp4`/`.mkv` files in the NGC-staged warehouse resource and baked into the in-container DS main config's `[source-list]` block (static stream-mode, the skill default per `references/pipeline-config.md`). All URLs use the `file://` (filesrc) protocol; the agent does not prompt the user for stream URLs and does not fabricate `rtsp://` URLs.
The agent starts `metropolis_perception_app` inside the running container (visible as a `docker exec` invocation or equivalent in the trajectory).
The agent reports the REST endpoint as `http://localhost:9000` in its final summary.
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.

expects.query: Stop rtvi-cv and clean up the deployment.
checks: The agent enters the TEARDOWN flow and does not start a new deployment.
The agent discovers running RTVI-CV containers via `docker ps` (filtering on `rtvicv-perception-docker` or the running image).
The agent asks the user which container(s) to stop before executing any destructive command when more than one matching container exists.
The agent executes `docker stop` (graceful by default) on the selected container(s).
NGC credentials at `~/.ngc/config` are preserved — the agent does not delete or chmod them away.
The engine cache directory and NGC-staged resources are NOT deleted unless the user explicitly requested a full cleanup.
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.

expects.query: Deploy rtvi-cv smartcity gdino with 2 streams and fakesink for benchmarking.
checks: The agent identifies the `smartcity-gdino` use case (Grounding DINO served by `nvinferserver`/Triton, with engine cached as a `.plan` file).

The agent resolves the RTVI-CV docker image and the GDINO model NGC reference by one of: (a) reading env vars / skill defaults, (b) reusing an already-available local RTVI-CV image on the host, OR (c) prompting the user.
The agent invokes `scripts/setup_gdino.sh` (or the equivalent in-container flow) to build/cache the Triton `.plan` engine before app start.
The DS main config applied inside the container uses `batch-size=2` and a `fakesink` output (matching the `for benchmarking` intent).

The 2 stream URLs are auto-discovered from `.mp4`/`.mkv` files in the NGC-staged smartcity resource and baked into the in-container DS main config's `[source-list]` block (static stream-mode, the skill default per `references/pipeline-config.md`). All URLs use the `file://` (filesrc) protocol; the agent does not prompt the user for stream URLs and does not fabricate `rtsp://` URLs.
The agent starts `metropolis_perception_app` inside the running container.
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.
The agent asks the user to select a use case from `warehouse-2d`, `warehouse-3d`, `smartcity-rtdetr`, or `smartcity-gdino` before proceeding.
The agent does NOT issue a `docker run` command until the use case is supplied.
The agent does NOT silently default to one of the four use cases.
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.
